title: Adityanath flags environmental concerns, launches 35-crore sapling drive
content: CM said that at Prime Minister Narendra Modi's request, under the 'Ek Pedh Maa Ke Naam' campaign, 5 crore saplings were planted this year on June 5, which is World Environment Day. More than 35 crore saplings were planted last year, bringing the total to 242 crore saplings planted in the state over the past nine years.

title: Punjab Congress: Why the Channi–Raja Warring leadership battle matters | Explained
content: Responding to the controversy, Mr. Warring said there should indeed be no “sleeper cell” or “compromised leader” within the Congress

title: Datia bypoll: Stage set for high-stakes battle as BJP, Congress candidates file nomination; Mishra gets emotional
content: The bypoll was necessitated after Congress MLA Rajendra Bharti lost his assembly membership following his conviction in a cheating case

title: The political storm in Punjab surrounding ‘Satluj’ movie | Explained
content: As the controversy surrounding the film and its removal continued unabated, political organisations, youth outfits and religious bodies have been organising public screenings of the film in villages and towns of Punjab

title: West Bengal CM supports curbs on Kolkata airport mosque entry
content: Airport authorities said that ‘namaz’ at the over-136-year-old Gouripur Jama Masjid, also called Bankra Mosque, inside the Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport was suspended for three days from Saturday (July 11, 2026) for the shrine’s renovation

title: EAM Jaishankar launches India’s UNSC campaign for 2028-29 term
content: Elections for the 2028-29 term will be held in June next year, when India and Tajikistan will compete for the sole seat in the Asia-Pacific Group

title: Survey for Bidadi Township ends in chaos as women farmers chase officials with brooms
content: With this, the row over Bidadi township, championed by Chief Minister D. K. Shivakumar and has become a political flashpoint with Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy, has only escalated further.

title: India, Japan review defence ties, prepare ground for 2+2 dialogue later this year
content: According to Ministry of Defence, the two sides assessed the progress made since the previous round of talks and reaffirmed their commitment to further strengthening the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership

title: Bankipur assembly by-election: Prashant Kishor and his wife declare total assets of over ₹208 crore
content: The poll strategist-turned-politician has eight criminal cases pending against him, with most of the cases dealing with unlawful assembly

title: Bihar Environment Minister inaugurates ‘Ecotourism Investors Meet-2026’
content: Aims to boost ecotourism with private investors’ participation for developing sites. Ecotourism destinations are divided into two groups for their development. Group A includes 29 ‘Reservoir-based ecotourism projects’ ; Group B includes 247 projects, including ponds, lakes, and wetlands

title: Fair, reasoned process must decide citizenship: Supreme Court
content: SC sets aside 27 judgments of Guahati High Court on foreigners, tells tribunals to adjudicate afresh; it says such declarations cannot be sustainable if “procedure adopted is mechanical, one-sided, or devoid of application of mind”

title: Chief Minister Vijay inaugurates MLA office in his Perambur constituency
content: He also inaugurated the e-Sevai (e-Service) centre on the premises, held discussions with officials on the integrated development projects for Perambur, and received petitions from members of the public

title: SRM Executive Education organises industry-academia round table
content: A total of 35 industry leaders across sectors such as FinTech, Global Capability Centres, automotive, hospitals, healthcare, consulting, Banking, cyber security participated in the round table

title: Why Tamil Nadu does not need privatisation of power distribution
content: Government funding narrows the gap between average cost of supply and average revenue realisation of the TNPDCL, says the White Paper

title: ED receives copies of documents in alleged ‘horse-trading’ case
content: The agency is likely to probe the money laundering angle in the case. The police had seized ₹60 lakh in unaccounted cash from one of the suspects

title: Kuki-Zo Council meets IB chief, seeks govt. action on blockade by Nagas
content: Kuki-Zo Council members say restoration of the popular government in Manipur on February 4, a year after President’s Rule was imposed, has been of “no good” to the people

title: Concern as district records five leptospirosis deaths in July
content: The district recording five leptospirosis deaths in the first 13 days of July, the highest in the State

title: Karnataka SIR: As distribution of enumeration forms enters final phase, political parties take on verification of ‘untraceable’ voters
content: As of July 13, the Election Commission has identified more than 9.37 lakh electors  absent, shifted, dead, duplicate or otherwise untraceable

title: Panel headed by Sudha Pillai to study Kerala Infrastructure Investment Fund Board revamp
content: The tenure of the five-member committee is three months from the date of the July 13 order. The panel will also examine the resource mobilisation strategy adopted by KIIFB

title: Assam Rifles jawan killed in Nagaland IED blast
content: The incident, which injured four others, occurred when a convoy of vehicles was returning from Sukhovi to Dimapur town

title: Nine bodies recovered from trawler that went missing off Bengal coast; six fishermen missing
content: The  , Joy Maa Kaali, set sail from Purba Medinipur; it went missing on July 2 and was found in the Sundarbans; the crew gave a distress call on July 8 about engine failure and sought help, says fishermen’s union

title: Saffron paint of Academy of Fine Arts security room sparks protest; theatre artists write to BJP Bengal chief
content: Since the BJP came to power in West Bengal in May, several government buildings, streets, and civic structures have been witnessing a colour change, including some sections of State secretariat Nabanna

title: Supreme Court refuses to hear Telangana’s plea against Polavaram project
content: The top court said that the suit could not be taken up for hearing until the Telangana government complied with the requisite procedural formalities

title: Flight carrying bodies of 15 Indians killed in Vietnam boat tragedy lands at Mumbai airport
content: Of the 15 victims, 10 were from Tamil Nadu, three were from Andhra Pradesh, and two were from Kerala. Two of the deceased were women.

title: India-Australia ‘administrative arrangements’ for uranium supplies | Explained
content: The recent agreement paves the way for Australian uranium exports to India, allowing private sector collaboration under stringent IAEA oversight

title: Can biogas aid India’s energy security?
content: India, which imports nearly 85% of its crude oil needs, remains vulnerable to global energy shocks. Over the years, it has pushed several initiatives to promote compressed biogas. Despite these efforts, progress has been limited; government support and incentives could provide a boost to the sector

title: BJP will lay siege to KRS reservoir if water is not released for standing crops, warns Leader of Opposition in Legislative Assembly R. Ashok
content: The government must immediately release water from the KRS reservoir and hold an all-party meeting to discuss the issue of sharing Cauvery water with Tamil Nadu, he said.

title: Supreme Court questions misuse of POCSO in teen relationships
content: Bench observes that parents often resort to criminal proceedings to protect their so-called ‘honour’ when teenage girls elope with their partners

title: Autonomous hill councils to be extended to all seven districts in Ladakh
content: The Ladakh administration has added 17 additional tehsils taking the total to 32; Chief Secretary Ashish Kundra says several rounds of discussions have been held with representatives of religious organisations and civil society bodies over constitutional safeguards

title: Tamil Nadu Government transfers DVAC chief A. Arun, appoints IG Mageshwari
content: A. Arun was appointed as the Director of the DVAC on May 25 and has now been transferred within two months of taking charge

title: IAS officers transferred in Tamil Nadu
content: TNMSC MD H. Krishnanunni posted as Director of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary Services; S.P. Amrith replaces Mr. Krishnaunni; Labour Commissioner Shilpa Prabhakar Satish replaces P. Umanath as MD of Tamil Nadu Civil Supplies Corporation

title: Mysuru: Free women’s oncology clinic, HPV vaccination drive to be organised on July 15
content: The clinic and vaccination drive will be held at the BHIO Day Care Clinic, Kuvempunagar, from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m.

title: Judicial custody of all eight accused in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case extended
content: Court of Additional District Judge Pratibha Narayan to decide the prosecution’s request for police remand of two of the eight accused on Tuesday

title: Amid Iran War, Centre Urged To Roll Out India's 1st LPG Efficiency Policy
content: US-Iran War: The proposal calls for awareness campaigns on efficient LPG usage, greater collaboration between the government, OMCs and stakeholders.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Indian Crew Member Killed After Iran Strikes 2 UAE Tankers In Hormuz
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at ending their war, which has caused global economic shockwaves since it began in late February.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Markets Likely To Open In Red As Oil Prices Hit $85-Mark
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices jump to one-month high after 10% rally; Brent oil near $85 a barrel.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Delhi Gets Warmer, Partly Cloudy Sky Expected Today
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Update: Delhi recorded a maximum temperature of 38.6 degrees Celsius on Monday

title: Delhi Finalises Eligibility For Rs 2,500 Women's Scheme: Who Can Apply?
content: Under the scheme, officially renamed Delhi Lakshmi Yojana, eligible women between the ages of 21 and 60 will receive Rs 2,500 every month. The government is yet to announce the registration process and launch date.

title: 'Mojtaba Khamenei Is 90% Gone': Trump's Big Claim As US Strikes Iran Again
content: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since February 28, when he was injured in the same US and Israeli strikes that also killed his father.

title: Telangana-Amazon Partner To Set Up Air-Conditioned Rest Stops For Gig Workers
content: The Telangana government in partnership with Amazon India, launched an initiative to provide essential facilities and improve the working environment of gig workers in the city.

title: Who Is Lindsey Graham's Sister, Chosen As Replacement After Senator's Death
content: Lindsey Graham's sister, Darline Graham Nordone, has been named as her late brother's temporary replacement in the US Senate after his unexpected death over the weekend.

title: 4 Boys Kill Watchman, Escape From Observation Home In Chhattisgarh
content: Four inmates of a government-run observation home, aged between 18 and over 20 years, allegedly strangled to death a 40-year-old watchman and escaped from the facility in Chhattisgarh's Bilaspur district.

title: Bodies Of 15 Indians Who Died In Vietnam Boat Tragedy Reach Mumbai
content: The tragedy struck on July 11 when a tourist speedboat carrying 36 people, including 32 Indian tourists and four Vietnamese crew members, capsized near Hon May Rut Ngoai Island off Vietnam's Phu Quoc Island.

title: Trump's Thursday Speech To Focus On 2020 Election Interference: Report
content: US President Donald Trump is slated to speak on Thursday about newly declassified intelligence on foreign nations' plans to interfere in the US election in 2020, an MS Now reporter said on X, citing two unidentified White House officials.

title: Trump Sends Congress Formal Notice That Iran Conflict Has Resumed
content: As the conflict intensified, Trump said on Monday, the US was reinstating its blockade of Iranian shipping in the Gulf and would ensure the Strait of Hormuz stays open.

title: US Launches Strikes On Iran For Third Consecutive Night
content: The US military said Monday it had launched a fresh salvo of attacks on Iran, marking the third night in a row that it had struck the country, as a deal to end the war falters.

title: Iran War: US Military Death Count Rises To 14, Over 400 Injured
content: Iran War: A total of 414 service members have been wounded, including a US Air Force member added Monday. While Iran and the US have resumed strikes, it is unclear if that is what led to the airman's injury.

title: Series Of Blasts Heard In Kyiv After Missile Alert
content: A series of explosions were heard in the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, shortly after midnight on Tuesday, an AFP journalist said, after the air force warned that several missiles were approaching.

title: US Immigration Agent Kills Colombian Man, 2nd Such Killing In A Week
content: A federal immigration officer fatally shot a Colombian man in Maine on Monday, marking the second time in a week that US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have used deadly force.

title: Yemen Claims Strike On Sanaa Airport, Houthis Attack Saudi Arabia
content: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis targeted Saudi Arabia on Monday, hours after the rebels accused the kingdom of attacking Sanaa airport -- the biggest flare-up in years between the two sides that threatens to upend a frozen conflict.

title: Donald Trump To Address Nation On Thursday
content: US President Donald Trump said he would make a primetime address to the nation on Thursday, in an announcement that comes amid a major flare-up of hostilities with Iran.

title: US To Resume Iran Port Blockade From Today Amid Escalating Tensions
content: The United States will begin enforcing its renewed blockade of Iran's ports on Tuesday at 2000 GMT, the US military said.

title: 'Responsibility to protect Americans': Trump tells US Congress fighting with Iran resumed
content: US President Trump has updated Congress on the recommencement of military action against Iran, marking the beginning of a new sixty-day operational phase for US forces. This escalation follows reported attacks on commercial shipping, attributed to Iran's supposed violation of a ceasefire. In response, the US has carried out several strikes, while Democrats assess potential legal actions to enforce compliance from the administration.

title: NCP rift out in open: Party secy challenges Sunetra’s takeover
content: A legal notice challenges Sunetra Pawar's election as national NCP president.  Praful Patel acknowledged a leadership vacuum and the need for corrective measures.  The notice questions the validity of the election process and constitutional protocols followed.  It also highlights inconsistencies in party submissions to the Election Commission.  The party has fifteen days to declare the election and office-bearer list null and void.

title: Team India look to shake off T20I hangover as road to ODI World Cup begins
content: After a disastrous T20I tour, India begin their ODI World Cup build-up against England at Edgbaston. With injuries forcing changes, Shubman Gill's side will look to reset, test combinations and lean on the experience of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma and Jasprit Bumrah as preparations for the 2027 World Cup gather pace.

title: 'Govt doesn’t care about your life': Mahua Moitra urges Wangchuk to call off hunger strike
content: Trinamool MP Mahua Moitra urged Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike.  Wangchuk's fast against examination irregularities reached sixteen days.  Eminent citizens also appealed for the protesters to end their fast.  They expressed concern over the protesters' worsening health conditions.  Activists question the government's lack of dialogue with protesters.

title: 'AI will create more jobs than it eliminates': Cisco president Jeetu Patel
content: Cisco president Jeetu Patel believes artificial intelligence will create more jobs than it eliminates. AI fluency will become a crucial skill, making some workers significantly more effective. Technology advancements lower costs, spurring new products and markets for growth. The future will offer more jobs, but their nature and required skills will change. Prioritizing AI skills development is essential for workforce preparedness.

title: India launches UNSC campaign for 2028-29 term, Jaishankar unveils ‘SHANTI’ vision
content: India officially launched its campaign for a United Nations Security Council seat. External affairs minister S Jaishankar highlighted the country's peacekeeping and development partnerships. India presented its SHANTI vision for global governance and cooperation among nations. The nation seeks support for its candidature amid a changing global landscape. India previously served eight terms as a non-permanent Security Council member.

title: Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma close in on all-time India ODI record in England
content: Virat Kohli needs 68 runs to surpass Rahul Dravid's tally of 648 runs. Rohit Sharma requires 239 runs to move ahead of Dravid's total. Dravid currently holds the record with 648 runs from 20 matches. This series presents a big chance for Kohli to claim the top spot.

title: Middle East crisis: Iran claims strike on US base; Bahrain sounds nationwide missile alert
content: In a significant escalation, two Emirati oil tankers were hit by Iranian cruise missiles in the Strait of Hormuz. Tragically, one Indian national lost their life, while eight crew members suffered injuries during the assault. Calling this act unacceptable, the United Arab Emirates pledged a robust response. Iran defended its actions by stating the ships disregarded warnings and disabled their navigation systems, amid ongoing military tensions with the United States.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: Egypt's richest family backs a $41 million plan that could change the Giza Pyramids forever
content: Egyptian billionaire Naguib Sawiris is investing more to redevelop the Giza Plateau. This significant investment aims to transform the historic site into a year-round tourist destination. The project includes adding electric buses and constructing a large open-air sports arena. Orascom Pyramids Entertainment expects concerts and sporting events to generate substantial revenue. The company anticipates breaking even on this ambitious venture by 2027.

title: Britain bought 1,525 acres nearly lifeless farmland, now beavers made it profitable
content: A farm in Lincolnshire is evolving into a lucrative wildland endeavor, with beavers playing a pivotal role in this ecological project. The initiative focuses on demonstrating that restoring nature can lead to substantial private income. Current revenue streams are generated through carbon removal and biodiversity credits, with aspirations to draw significant investments for sustainable environmental solutions.

title: Moody’s chief economist Mark Zandi sees 'big warning' signs in June Jobs report for the US
content: Economist Mark Zandi believes the June US employment report presents an overly optimistic view.  He noted modest payroll gains and downward revisions to prior months' job increases.  The household survey showed a sharp decline in employment, which Zandi highlighted.  He argued the falling unemployment rate is misleading due to declining labor force participation.  Other economists also expressed concerns about the labor market data's interpretation.

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's adorable toy car moment goes viral
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's playful interaction with a miniature electric car has captured public attention. This adorable moment occurred during a meeting with officials from VinFast, a Vietnamese electric vehicle maker. The viral clip shows Vijay examining the tiny car with a warm smile, delighting his fans. Meanwhile, anticipation for his upcoming film 'Jana Nayagan' continues to grow among audiences.

title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock market recommendations for July 14 - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Divi's Laboratories, Bajaj Finserv, Bharat Forge, and Coforge are the top stocks to buy recommended by Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research at Mirae Asset Sharekhan for July 14, 2026:

title: 1 killed, 7 injured as boiler explodes at steel melting unit in Tamil Nadu
content: A furnace boiler exploded at a steel melting unit in Gummidipoondi, killing one worker. Seven other guest workers sustained severe burn injuries in the powerful blast. Molten metal splashed onto the injured workers during the incident. Fire and rescue services controlled the blaze and rescued the injured. Police are investigating the cause of the fatal explosion.

title: Bruins, Flyers linked to trade for Kraken forward Shane Wright
content: The Seattle Kraken’s star player Shane Wright ended the last season with 12 goals and 27 points in 74 games, sparking concern about him. It seems this has led him to actively seek trade from the team as the Seattle kraken is reportedly actively working to move him. Amid this, the Boston Bruins and the Philadelphia Flyers have been linked to him.

title: Chiefs and Packers emerge as favourites for $120m star wide receiver
content: The Miami Dolphins’ star player Tyreek Hill has experienced a pretty challenging time in the last few months amid his brutal knee injury, sparking chaos among many. Despite his record breaking performance in 2024, Tyreek Hill continues to remain a free agent, ahead of the new season. Amid this, the Kansas City Chiefs and the Green Bay Packers have emerged as potential landing spots.

title: Streamer claims Netanyahu aides pitched gym livestream for younger voters
content: A streamer named Clavicular claims Netanyahu's advisers sought his help. They reportedly wanted a gym livestream to improve the leader's image. Clavicular stated advisers believed Netanyahu appeared old and out of shape. This claim remains unverified by any official confirmation. A meeting with an adviser was confirmed at a nightclub.

title: NEET UG 2026 recorded responses released at neet.nta.nic.in; NTA opens OMR challenge window
content: The National Testing Agency has released the scanned OMR answer sheets and recorded responses for NEET UG 2026 re-examination candidates. Applicants can review their records and raise online objections against recorded responses until 11.00 am on July 15, 2026, by paying a processing fee of Rs 200 per challenge. NTA has clarified that the answer key challenge process has already ended, and no further objections against the answer key will be accepted.

title: RSCIT admit card 2026 released at vmou.ac.in for July and August exams
content: RSCIT admit card 2026: VMOU, Kota has released the RSCIT admit cards for candidates appearing in the July and August 2026 examination cycle. Candidates scheduled for the July 19 and August 2 exams can download their hall tickets from the official Permission Letter portal using their login credentials. The article includes the direct download link, step-by-step instructions to access the admit card, alternative download options through IT Gyan Kendras, required documents and the RSCIT examination pattern.

title: Delhi constable shoots wife dead hours after her birthday; family alleges dowry harassment
content: A Delhi Police constable allegedly shot his wife dead after a heated argument early Monday.  The couple had left their home following an altercation and were reportedly heading to a hospital.  CCTV footage captured the couple arguing moments before the constable opened fire with his service pistol.  He then fled the scene, leaving his wife critically injured on the roadside.  Police are currently conducting raids to apprehend the absconding constable.

title: 'Sorry': Why Brendon McCullum apologised to fans ahead of IND vs ENG 1st ODI
content: Brendon McCullum has apologised to England fans after his removal as Test coach. He accepted the decision, stating the team's results were not good enough. McCullum admitted disappointment over failing to win major series against India and Australia. He took full responsibility for the team's culture and tactical performances. The former New Zealand captain will continue as England's white-ball coach.

title: US launches third straight night of strikes on Iran as Hormuz tensions escalate
content: United States forces conducted strikes on Iranian military assets for a third night. These actions aim to degrade Iran's ability to attack commercial shipping. President Trump announced plans to reinstate a blockade on Iranian shipping. The Strait of Hormuz has become a focal point of US-Iran confrontation. A maritime blockade of Iranian ports will take effect Tuesday.

title: One Indian crew member killed, 6 injured after Iran strikes UAE tankers in Hormuz
content: Two UAE-flagged tankers were struck by Iranian cruise missiles in Omani waters. One Indian crew member died and eight others sustained injuries in the attack. The UAE condemned the incident as a serious violation of international law. This escalation occurs amid rising military tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. The US Central Command also launched new strikes against Iran.

title: 'We were talking about...': Gill opens up on 2027 WC planning with Kohli
content: Indian captain Shubman Gill advocates for the 50-over format's preservation. He suggests reviving triangular or quadrangular series to enhance engagement. Gill also congratulated the women's team on their historic Test victory. He is planning for the 2027 ODI World Cup with Virat Kohli. Jasprit Bumrah's return to the ODI squad is welcomed by Gill.

title: Imtiaz Ali on daughter Ida's engagement: 'It is the greatest happiness
content: Filmmaker Imtiaz Ali's daughter, Ida Ali, is now engaged to her boyfriend, Krish Agrawal. The couple announced their engagement after a proposal during their trip to Norway. Krish Agrawal works as a quantitative researcher at a hedge fund, and they have been together long. Imtiaz Ali expressed his happiness and confirmed Krish sought his blessing before proposing.

title: 'Comprehensive sex education' coming soon to schools, govt tells Supreme Court
content: India will formally introduce comprehensive sex education in schools and colleges nationwide. The government has accepted a committee's report recommending this significant curriculum change. This move aims to address concerns regarding adolescent pregnancies and criminalization under the Pocso Act. Subjects like child sexual abuse and safety will be integrated from foundational stages. The National Council of Educational Research and Training will develop the new curriculum.

title: LeT, IS operatives among 34 killed in Khuzdar attack: BLA
content: The Baloch Liberation Army claimed a major suicide attack on an ISIS-K commander's compound. This operation targeted a meeting coordinating efforts against the Baloch political movement. The BLA stated that 34 operatives, including ISIS and LeT members, were killed. The attack, executed by the Majeed Brigade, followed weeks of intelligence surveillance. This incident highlights alleged links between Pakistan's ISI and militant groups.

title: ‘Democratic decentralisation’: Govt announces hill councils for all 7 Ladakh districts
content: Ladakh will establish hill development councils in all seven districts to strengthen grassroots governance. This move aims to decentralize power and improve administrative reach across the vast territory. Currently, only Leh and Kargil districts have these autonomous district councils. Discussions are ongoing for a unique UT-level legislative body with broad powers. A draft framework for this new arrangement will be prepared soon.

title: India's trade deficit hits 5-month high as imports surge
content: India's trade deficit reached a five-month high as imports surged significantly. Exports showed continued growth, but were overshadowed by increased import costs. Crude oil and fertilizer imports saw substantial year-on-year expansion in June. Electronics and engineering goods led export growth, while gold imports remained stable. Analysts anticipate a widening current account deficit for the fiscal year.

title: Sam Neill at 78: Marriage to Noriko, love story with Lisa Harrow
content: Legendary New Zealand actor Sam Neill, best known for playing Dr. Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, has died at the age of 78. Neill was known for keeping his personal life private. He married Japanese makeup artist Noriko Watanabe in 1989 after the pair met while working on Dead Calm, which also starred Nicole Kidman. Before his marriage, Neill was in a long-term relationship with New Zealand actress Lisa Harrow, his Omen III co-star.

title: Supreme Court quashes Guwahati high court order on 27 'foreigners'
content: The Supreme Court stressed fair procedures for declaring individuals foreign nationals. It found fault with tribunals and the Guwahati High Court's actions. Many orders were quashed as they lacked hearings for affected people. The court directed tribunals to provide fresh hearings and reconsider cases. This ensures lawful adjudication and protects constitutional rights for all.

title: Kharif acreage falls 16%, but ethanol effect boosts sugarcane
content: Overall kharif crop acreage has fallen sixteen percent as of last Friday. Sugarcane acreage, however, has increased despite water resource pressures. Pulses and cereals show the highest decline in sown area this season. Farmers continue to favor cash crops for better market prices. This trend supports government ethanol initiatives and reduces import bills.

title: UK: Peterborough council denies sale of Hindu temple to mosque is ‘unlawful’
content: Peterborough city council argued its decision to sell a complex housing a Hindu temple was not unlawful. The council stated that while Hindu faith is protected, it does not dictate decisions. The Hindu community expressed disbelief at the council's valuation of alternative premises. The council needs the money from the sale to the UK Islamic Mission. Bharat Hindu Samaj is seeking a judicial review to quash the council's decision.

title: Ram Mandir Trust invites CEO applications: Must be a 'practising Hindu, Ram bhakt'
content: The Ram Janmbhoomi Teerth Kshetra trust is seeking applications for its first chief executive officer. This professional role aims to enhance the management of the Ayodhya Ram temple operations. The trust decided to appoint a CEO after recent allegations of donation theft by cash-counting agents. Applications are open for eligible Hindu candidates with extensive managerial experience. The new CEO will report to the trust's general secretary and oversee daily functions.

title: Supreme Court seeks Ram mandir fund probe status report in a week
content: The Supreme Court is now overseeing the Ayodhya Ram Mandir donation embezzlement probe. It has directed the UP government's Special Investigation Team to submit a status report. The court is considering multiple public interest litigations seeking independent investigations. The probe has already led to the arrest of eight individuals involved in suspicious activities. Further hearings on this matter are scheduled for July 20.

title: AAP launches signature drive to seek death for Ayodhya donation thieves
content: Arvind Kejriwal has initiated a nationwide signature campaign demanding capital punishment for those accused of theft. He launched the drive in Delhi, signing an open letter to Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Kejriwal urged citizens to organize Hanuman Chalisa recitations and gather signatures for the letter. Delhi BJP functionary Praveen Kapoor stated the campaign aims to defame the Ram Mandir.

title: US to resume naval blockade of Iranian ports, warns vessels of interception
content: The United States will restart enforcing a maritime blockade on Iran from Tuesday. This action targets all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports and coastal areas. American forces will enforce the blockade against vessels transiting to or from Iranian ports. Commercial mariners have been advised to monitor official navigation notices and contact naval forces. The renewed blockade follows days of military escalation around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Anti-graft bills not aimed at undercutting non-BJP governments, says home ministry
content: The home ministry has rejected opposition claims regarding anti-corruption bills. These proposed laws aim to remove arrested officials after thirty days in custody. Legislative membership remains intact, ensuring government stability is unaffected. The ministry asserts the mandate is protected by ensuring effective and trustworthy governance. Parliamentary committees are scrutinizing these proposed anti-corruption measures.

title: Citing former PM Manmohan Singh, BJP says Congress has habit of discrediting EC
content: The BJP cited Manmohan Singh's remarks to accuse Congress of attacking institutions. Congress countered by referencing Quraishi's book to criticize Prime Minister Modi. The BJP alleged ministers targeted the poll body when the UPA was in power. Salman Khurshid was censured for promising an enhanced Muslim quota during a campaign. Singh viewed the EC as democracy's soul, while Modi sees it as a political tool.

title: Jammu and Kashmir: BJP slaps Rs 100 crore notice on Omar over MLA poaching charge
content: The BJP has issued a Rs 100-crore defamation notice to Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah. This action follows Abdullah's allegations that a BJP functionary attempted to poach his party MLA. The notice demands an unconditional public apology from Abdullah within seven days. Abdullah dismissed the notice, viewing it as a sign of his political significance. He also warned of legal counter-notices against BJP functionaries for their past statements.

title: Rapper TI announces retirement after releasing his 12th album, Kill the King
content: Rapper TI has officially announced his retirement from the music scene following the release of his twelfth album, 'Kill the King.' Embracing a lifestyle that leans towards family time, he feels it's time to step back. TI expressed heartfelt gratitude to his fans for their support with this final endeavor, which was inspired by the wisdom of Big Boi.

title: SAD leader shot in Mansa: Sukhbir aide Dalvir Kala injured in attack; hospitalised
content: Shiromani Akali Dal leader Dalvir Singh Kala was shot and injured by an assailant. The incident occurred in Punjab's Mansa district on Monday evening. Kala, a former Zila Parishad member, was attacked near his shop. Police are investigating the shooting and examining CCTV footage. Efforts are underway to identify and apprehend the motorcycle-borne attacker.

title: 12 districts in A.P. get new DMHOs
content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more . Unlock these with Subscription Products you've access to Additional Subscription Benefits Account Settings Need help with your subscription? content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more . title: Meet the magnetic liquid metal robot that merges & squeezes through tiny gaps
